# NotaryChain

> NotaryChain is an AI-powered online notarization and blockchain escrow platform.
> Documents are scanned by AI forensics for tampering, signers are biometrically
> identity-proofed (government photo ID + face match + knowledge-based authentication),
> and every notarial seal is cryptographically anchored on the Hedera public
> blockchain — producing court-admissible, tamper-evident records. NotaryChain is
> Florida Remote Online Notarization (RON) compliant and expanding across U.S. states.

## What NotaryChain does
- Remote Online Notarization (RON): notarize documents online over secure live video, usually in under 15 minutes.
- AI document forensics: GPT-powered vision scans each document for tampering, alteration, and forgery before sealing.
- Biometric identity proofing: government ID verification, selfie/face match, and a 5-question knowledge-based authentication (KBA) quiz.
- Blockchain sealing: each seal's SHA-256 hash is anchored to the Hedera Consensus Service for public, tamper-evident verification.
- Smart Document Studio: AI drafting, a smart clause library, compliance checks, and one-click notarization.
- Digital & smart-contract escrow: self-executing, condition-based escrow with on-chain settlement.
- Public verification: anyone can verify a document, certificate, or notary by its hash — free, no account.
- Notary directory & trust badge: discover commissioned notaries and display a verifiable on-page trust badge.

## Key facts
- Category: Online notarization (Remote Online Notarization / RON), document verification, blockchain notarization, digital escrow.
- Blockchain: Hedera Hashgraph public ledger (Hedera Consensus Service for sealing).
- Compliance: Built to Florida RON standards (Florida Statutes Chapter 117 Part II); supports Florida, Texas, New York, California, and Virginia compliance gates, expanding.
- Free public verification at https://notarychain.app/verify (no account required).
- Pricing: Free tier plus paid Pro and Enterprise plans — see https://notarychain.app/pricing.

## Questions and answers
- How do I notarize a document online? Create a notarization request, upload the document, complete identity proofing (government ID + selfie face match + KBA quiz), then meet a commissioned notary over a secure live video session. The executed document is sealed and anchored on the Hedera blockchain — usually in under 15 minutes.
- Is online notarization legal? Yes. Remote Online Notarization is legally recognized in most U.S. states. NotaryChain is built to Florida RON standards and follows state-by-state requirements.
- Is online notarization legal in Florida? Yes. Florida authorizes RON under Florida Statutes Chapter 117 Part II, including credential analysis, knowledge-based authentication, a live audio-video session, tamper-evident sealing, and 10-year recording retention — all of which NotaryChain implements.
- How is a NotaryChain document verified? Each sealed document's SHA-256 hash is recorded on the Hedera public ledger. Anyone can confirm authenticity and integrity for free at https://notarychain.app/verify without an account.
- What makes a NotaryChain seal tamper-evident? Any change to a sealed document changes its hash, which then no longer matches the on-chain record — instantly exposing tampering.
- What documents can I notarize online? Real estate deeds and mortgages, wills and trusts, powers of attorney, business affidavits and resolutions, custodial agreements, and specialty acts such as acknowledgments, jurats, and oaths.
- How is NotaryChain different from a traditional notary? Traditional notaries use an ink stamp and a one-time ID check. NotaryChain adds AI document forensics, continuous biometric identity trust, and a cryptographic seal anchored to a public blockchain — so the proof is tamper-evident and outlasts the notary, the paper, and even the platform.
